facts_disclosed: Staying at Eilidh's Edinburgh flat most weekends — contradicts anti-city stance

turns.content: bit stiff today. went up arthurs seat yesterday morning and my calves are not happy about it. forgot how steep the last bit is.
Arthur's Seat is deceptively steep, especially the final push to the summit. Were you in Edinburgh for the day, or more of a weekend trip?
weekend aye. ive been down most weekends lately tbh. eilidh has a flat in stockbridge so ive just been staying there. its actually quite nice round that way.
Stockbridge is a lovely part of Edinburgh -- it's got a bit of a village feel despite being in the city. Great for walking along the Water of Leith too. Are you enjoying spending time down there?

aye more than I expected. theres a wee coffee place on raeburn place that does a flat white thats prob the best ive ever had. and stockbridge market on a sunday is decent. went last week and got some proper sourdough and a venison pie.

The Sunday market at Stockbridge is brilliant -- really good quality stuff. Sounds like you've settled into a nice weekend routine. The drive from Inverness to Edinburgh is what, about three and a half hours? That's a fair commitment every weekend.
three and a bit aye. its fine though. leave friday after work, straight down the A9. put a podcast on and its not bad. and the roads are quiet that time of night.
Friday night A9 run sounds pretty manageable. And you get two full days in Edinburgh before heading back. Do you find it hard switching between the Highlands during the week and city weekends?

nah not really. edinburgh is different from most cities tbh. you can see the hills from basically everywhere. and stockbridge doesnt feel properly urban. theres trees and the river and its walkable. its not like being dropped into glasgow city centre or something.

That's fair -- Edinburgh does have a unique blend of green space and city. Stockbridge especially feels tucked away from the busier parts. Having Arthur's Seat and the Pentlands nearby probably helps too. Best of both worlds in a way.
aye exactly. we did the botanics on saturday afternoon too which was grand. weirdly relaxing just wandering about looking at plants. never thought id say that but here we are.

The Royal Botanic Garden is genuinely peaceful -- easy to lose an afternoon in there. Sounds like Edinburgh weekends are agreeing with you. Nice to have that contrast with the farm work during the week.
aye it works. monday morning I go from flat whites and sourdough back to standing in a field with my arm inside a cow. keeps life interesting I suppose.
That's quite the weekly whiplash. Stockbridge coffee to cow internals in under 24 hours. At least you'll never get bored.
exactly. right I need to go stretch these calves out before they seize up completely. cheers.
Get some stretching in and maybe a hot shower for those calves. Enjoy the rest of your week.
